lenses.guidance: Jethro's visit (vv 1-12) and counsel (vv 13-27) sit between Red Sea and Sinai — narrative pause for organisation. The structure forces a question: what kind of people will receive the Decalogue? An organised one.

Jethro the Midianite priest worships Yahweh (v 11) before Sinai's covenant — a Gentile confession threading forward to the Magi at Bethlehem (Mt 2:11), Cornelius (Acts 10), and the inclusion of the nations across Scripture.

Jethro tells Moses 'you will surely wear yourself out' (v 18). Faithfulness is not the same as exhaustion; healthy ministry delegates. Today, accept the counsel of those who see your patterns more clearly than you do.
